name: Blueberry-Orange Pie and Orange Crust
recipeIngredient: 7 cups fresh blueberries (rinsed and well drained)
1 recipe Orange pastry crust or crust for 9-inch, pie
recipeInstructions:
FILLING
1. Stir together sugar and cornstarch in a heavy-bottomed 3-quart saucepan.
2. Stir in orange juice concentrate, orange juice, and 2 cups of the blueberries.
3. Cook and stir over medium-high heat until the mixture thickens, becomes translucent, and just comes to a boil (7-10 minutes).
4. Remove from heat and gently fold in the remaining blueberries.
5. Mound the filling into the baked orange pastry crust.
6. Refrigerate for at least one hour before serving. Can be made up to one day ahead.
Makes one 9-inch pie, 8-10 servings.
CRUST
1. Combine flour, orange zest, sugar, and salt in a mixing bowl; stir to blend.
2. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut shortening into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse meal.
3. Combine orange juice with cold water.
4. Add the orange juice/water mixture by tablespoons, mixing gently with a fork just until the dough begins to hold together in clumps.
5. If necessary, add an extra tablespoon of water.
6. Gather the dough and shape into a flat round disk. Chill for at least 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
On lightly floured wax paper, roll out the dough to about 1/8 inch thick, forming a large enough circle to overhang the pie plate by 1 1/2 inches to 2 inches.
(A quick and accurate measure can be attained by turning the pie plate upside down onto the pastry and then adding 2 inches).
Ease the pastry into the pie plate, being careful not to stretch the dough. Fold the pastry under at the edge. Crimp decoratively.
Pick the bottom and sides.
Freeze briefly (15-20 minutes) before baking for 15 minutes or until golden brown.
Cool on a wire rack. Makes one pie crust.
